interrupts.name: RESET
interrupts.caption: External Pin, Power-on Reset, Brown-out Reset and Watchdog Reset

VADMUX.caption: Analog Channel and Gain Selection Bits

VADCSR.caption: The VADC Control and Status register

BGD.caption: Setting the BGD bit to one will disable the bandgap voltage reference. This bit must be cleared before enabling CC-ADC or V-ADC, and must remain unset while either ADC is enabled.

BGCC.caption: BG Calibration of PTAT Current Bits

SPMCSR.caption: Store Program Memory Control and Status Register

OSICSR.caption: Oscillator Sampling Interface Control and Status Register

PA1DID.caption: When this bit is written logic one, the digital input buffer of the corresponding V_ADC pin is disabled.

PA0DID.caption: When this bit is written logic one, the digital input buffer of the corresponding V_ADC pin is disabled.

BPCHCD.caption: Battery Protection Charge-High-current Detection Level Register

BPDHCD.caption: Battery Protection Discharge-High-current Detection Level Register

BPCOCD.caption: Battery Protection Charge-Over-current Detection Level Register

BPDOCD.caption: Battery Protection Discharge-Over-current Detection Level Register

BPSCD.caption: Battery Protection Short-Circuit Detection Level Register

DOCIF.caption: Discharge Over-current Protection Activated Interrupt Flag

COCIF.caption: Charge Over-current Protection Activated Interrupt Flag

DOCIE.caption: Discharge Over-current Protection Activated Interrupt Enable

COCIE.caption: Charge Over-current Protection Activated Interrupt Enable

ERASE_AND_WRITE_IN_ONE_OPERATION.caption: Erase and Write in one operation

ICIE1.caption: Timer/Counter n Input Capture Interrupt Enable

OCIE1B.caption: Timer/Counter1 Output Compare B Interrupt Enable

OCIE1A.caption: Timer/Counter1 Output Compare A Interrupt Enable

ICIE0.caption: Timer/Counter n Input Capture Interrupt Enable

CADCSRA.caption: CC-ADC Control and Status Register A

CADEN.caption: When the CADEN bit is cleared (zero), the CC-ADC is disabled. When the CADEN bit is set (one), the CC-ADC will continuously measure the voltage drop over the external sense resistor RSENSE. In Power-down, only the Regular Current detection is active. In Power-off, the CC-ADC is always disabled.

CADSI.caption: The CADSI bits determine the current sampling interval for the Regular Current detection in Power-down mode. The actual settings remain to be determined.

CADSE.caption: When the CADSE bit is written to one, the ongoing CC-ADC conversion is aborted, and the CC-ADC enters Regular Current detection mode.

CADCSRB.caption: CC-ADC Control and Status Register B

EESAVE.caption: Preserve EEPROM through the Chip Erase cycle
Preserve EEPROM through the Chip Erase cycle
